content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 11, 1987


Samuel Fineman
President
Bel-Air Door Company
322 South Date Avenue
P.O. Box 829
Alhambra, CA  91802-0829

Re:  Your Request for Informal
     Assistance
     Our File No. I-87-310

Dear Mr. Fineman:

     We have received your letter regarding Assemblyman Willie
Brown's recent announcement that he will serve as the campaign
manager for Jesse Jackson's presidential campaign.  You have
asked whether Assemblyman Brown is required to take an unpaid
leave of absence from the Legislature while managing the
presidential campaign.

     The Fair Political Practices Commission has jurisdiction
only over matters governed by the Political Reform Act
(Government Code Sections 81000-91015). There is no provision
in the Political Reform Act that would require Assemblyman
Brown to take an unpaid leave of absence from the Legislature
while managing the presidential campaign.  We are unable to
advise you whether there are other laws which might apply to
this situation.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]

                                        By:  Kathryn E. Donovan
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916)322-5660

Bel-Air Door Co.

December 4, 1987

THE FAIR POLITICAL
PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

With regard to my inquiry to Governor Deukmejian,
about Assemblyman Willie Brown's announcement that he
will be serving as Jessie Jackson's Presidential Campaign Manager. I should like to know the following.

How can a State Assemblyman accept a job that will
take him away from his responsibilities here in
California, where the people are paying his salary?
Of course, if he is taking off from the job, he should
take a leave of absence without pay and benefits and
expenses.

Thanking you in advance for your reply.

Sincerely,

BEL-AIR DOOR CO.

[signature]

Samuel Fineman
President

SF/gt

Enclosure

cc: Governor Deukmejian

322 So. Date Avenue • P.O. Box 829 • Alhambra, Calif. 91802-0829 • U.S.A / (213) 283-3731 • (818) 576-2545

State of California

GOVERNOR'S OFFICE
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN
GOVERNOR

TELEPHONE
(916) 445-2841

November 25, 1987


Mr. Samuel Fineman
President
Bel-Air Door Co.
P.O. Box 829
Alhambra, Ca  91802-0829

Dear Mr. Fineman:

Thank you for your letter to Governor Deukmejian
regarding Assemblyman Willie Brown's recent announcement
that he will serve as the new Campaign Manager for Jesse
Jackson's Presidential campaign.

Although the Governor appreciates your concern he is
not in a position to answer your legal questions.  The Fair
Political Practices Commission can best advise you on the
legalities of Assemblyman Brown's conduct.  They may be
contacted at 428 J Street, Suite 800, Sacramento, CA 95804
or 916/ 322-5600.

Thank you again for writing and please accept the
Governor's best wishes for a joyous holiday season.

Most cordially,

Margaret A. Bengs
Assistant to the Governor
for Constituent Affairs

sections.question: You have asked whether Assemblyman Brown is required to take an unpaid leave of absence from the Legislature while managing the presidential campaign.
sections.conclusion: There is no provision in the Political Reform Act that would require Assemblyman Brown to take an unpaid leave of absence from the Legislature while managing the presidential campaign. We are unable to advise you whether there are other laws which might apply to this situation.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Assemblyman Willie Brown is required to take an unpaid leave of absence from the Legislature while serving as campaign manager for Jesse Jackson's presidential campaign.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has no jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act to require an unpaid leave of absence for Assemblyman Brown, and cannot advise on whether other laws might apply.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes multiple related items: original citizen inquiry to Governor, Governor's referral to FPPC, and FPPC's informal assistance response letter dated December 11, 1987. FPPC letter is the primary advice letter with clear Q/C structure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked whether Assemblyman Brown is required to take an unpaid leave of absence from the Legislature while managing the presidential campaign.

CONCLUSION: There is no provision in the Political Reform Act that would require Assemblyman Brown to take an unpaid leave of absence from the Legislature while managing the presidential campaign. We are unable to advise you whether there are other laws which might apply to this situation.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides informal advice declining jurisdiction over a question about whether Assemblyman Willie Brown must take unpaid leave while serving as campaign manager for Jesse Jackson's presidential campaign. The FPPC concludes the Political Reform Act contains no such requirement and cannot advise on other potential legal restrictions.
